overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, with a total following of 3580. Engagement varies across platforms, with LinkedIn showing the highest individual post engagement based on likes and comments. The content focuses on research in social media analysis, information operations, and related academic opportunities. Recent activity indicates consistent posting across platforms.

contentThemes.description: Sharing updates on research projects, publications, and presentations.
Posting about postdoctoral positions and PhD opportunities in the field.
Discussing research related to detecting and analyzing coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media.
Sharing insights on methodological approaches to studying disinformation.

recommendations: Cross-promote content across platforms to increase visibility and engagement.
Use platform-specific features (e.g., LinkedIn articles, Mastodon polls, Bluesky advanced features) to enhance content.
Engage more actively with followers by responding to comments and questions.
Use relevant hashtags consistently to improve content discoverability.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., videos, infographics) to cater to diverse audience preferences.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows good engagement with research announcements and opportunities for students. Reposts are a significant driver of visibility on this platform.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon engagement is lower compared to other platforms. Focus on sharing more engaging content and participating in relevant conversations.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the highest engagement, particularly with job postings and grant announcements. Continue sharing professional updates and opportunities on this platform.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
